title: Partial model squeeze and IDMAC write-readback triage

findings.finding: The long four-lane batch was stopped after Strix produced two outputs. The completed outputs repeated closed descriptor alignment/coherency/ring-shape hypotheses and suggested SDHCI paths that do not apply to the sunxi-mmc driver.
findings.decision: Do not promote these suggestions to a kernel patch.

findings.finding: Existing H036 UART evidence already shows the IDMAC enable writes stick before the command: dma-exit has GCTRL=0x20000030, DMAC=0x00000282, IDIE=0x00000002, and DLBA=0x3f840000. The later stall still reaches IDST=0x00004000 DESC_READ with CHDA=DLBA, CBDA=0, and descriptor ownership unchanged.
findings.decision: A simple register write-readback or delay after DMAC enable is not a strong next behavior patch by itself.

findings.finding: No public archive reply was visible for the maintainer/list question during this pass.
findings.decision: Keep monitoring. Next model work should use one model lane per command with shorter timeouts to avoid long session stalls.

conclusion: No patch was made. H040 reduces the current speculative queue by closing the plain IDMAC write-readback idea against existing evidence.
